name: C.w.gregg's Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. To test the heat of chili pods, take a small nibble of dried chili in your mouth; when cooked, its strength will increase.
2. Brown hamburger meat in a large skillet over medium heat until thoroughly browned and fat is rendered.
3. Remove the meat from the skillet and set aside. In the same skillet, create the chili paste by removing some seeds from 6-8 dried chili pods, leaving others for flavor. Discard stems and blend with about half a cup of warm water (or just enough to form a paste) until smooth but not too wet.
4. Add oregano, cumin, and garlic cloves to the skillet; stir until well combined. Season with salt to taste.
5. Brown the mixture over medium heat for a few minutes, ensuring it doesn't burn.
6. Gradually add 4 tablespoons of flour, stirring continuously until incorporated and browned.
7. In another large saucepan or pot, combine tomato sauce and red wine; bring to a simmer.
8. Return the browned meat mixture to the saucepan and stir gently. Simmer for at least three hours (or longer) on low heat, or use a slow cooker if preferred.
9. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt as needed, along with additional cumin and oregano to suit your preference. Note that these flavors intensify during cooking; cumin may add a subtle sweetness.
10. If the chili is too hot upon completion, stir in more tomato sauce before serving.
11. Serve with sour cream, grated mild cheddar cheese, or any desired accompaniment.
12. Pair with iced tea for a refreshing beverage to cool down your mouth.
